message: fix(ci): remove SwiftPM manifest db WAL

files.patch:
@@ -89,9 +89,10 @@ jobs:
           set -euo pipefail
           # SwiftPM caches evaluated manifests in an sqlite DB. We've seen this get stale across
           # `swift-configuration` trait renames (e.g. `JSONSupport` -> `JSON`) and break resolution.
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
           # SwiftPM traits can be persisted into `.swiftpm/configuration/traits.json` (and can get stuck in caches).
           # When upstream packages rename/remove traits, stale state can break builds.
           find ~/.swiftpm -type f -name traits.json -print -delete || true
@@ -191,9 +192,10 @@ jobs:
           set -euo pipefail
           # SwiftPM caches evaluated manifests in an sqlite DB. We've seen this get stale across
           # `swift-configuration` trait renames (e.g. `JSONSupport` -> `JSON`) and break resolution.
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
           # SwiftPM traits can be persisted into `.swiftpm/configuration/traits.json` (and can get stuck in caches).
           # When upstream packages rename/remove traits, stale state can break builds.
           find ~/.swiftpm -type f -name traits.json -print -delete || true
@@ -298,9 +300,10 @@ jobs:
           set -euo pipefail
           # SwiftPM caches evaluated manifests in an sqlite DB. We've seen this get stale across
           # `swift-configuration` trait renames (e.g. `JSONSupport` -> `JSON`) and break resolution.
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db" || true
-          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db" || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/Library/Caches/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/manifests/manifest.db"* || true
+          rm -f "$HOME/.cache/org.swift.swiftpm/package-collection.db"* || true
           # SwiftPM traits can be persisted into `.swiftpm/configuration/traits.json` (and can get stuck in caches).
           # When upstream packages rename/remove traits, stale state can break builds.
           find ~/.swiftpm -type f -name traits.json -print -delete || true
